title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Gate Lens: Risk Profile
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates risk profile increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Risk Profile evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-risk-profile-1for9z: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Risk Profile into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-risk-profile-1for9z:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Risk Profile model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
